1.14: The firm may not act if the person dealing with the transaction or their immediate family is the seller.
1.15: The firm may act if the person dealing with the transaction or their immediate family is the borrower, provided no conflict of interest exists.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's solicitor details is not required.
The conveyancer must ensure the seller's solicitor is registered with a regulatory body and holds a current Practising Certificate.
3.1.4: Unrepresented sellers are acceptable provided the conveyancer has no concerns and the vendor supplies all necessary legal documentation.
3.1.5: Acceptable identity documents include: UK Photo Driving Licence with current address.
Government issued Benefit Entitlement Letter dated within the last 12 months.
HMRC letter dated within the last 12 months showing full NI number or unique tax reference code.

Certificate of Title must only be sent after the re-inspection is completed.
4.5b: Certificate of Title must be sent via Lenders Exchange on the customer offer link.
4.1: Valuation reports are only required for special schemes: Shared Ownership, Shared Equity, or Discounted Market Value/Sale.
The valuation figure must not be disclosed to the applicant(s).
4.3: Matters affecting value must be referred to the Issuing Office.
5.1.1: Mortgage Loan Services, PO Box 1477, Sunderland, SR5 9XE
5.2.1: Sub-sales on new-build developments are not acceptable for Buy to Let transactions.

Residential sub-sales require: Disclosure of Incentives Form (DIF) from original sale, LTV <= 70% of market value/purchase price, total mortgage less than third-party contracted price, deposit from own resources or family gift, and no relationship between applicant and vendor/third-party.
5.4.4: Do not provide desktop environmental searches or reports with pending recommendations.
Japanese Knotweed: Only advise if treatment is not covered by a valid, transferable, 10-year insurance-backed guarantee.
Infrastructure: Advise only if subject to compulsory purchase, demolition orders, or compensation.
Coal Mining: Advise if untreated/pre-1950 treated mine entry is within 20 meters.
Mundic: Reports must be less than 6 years old.
Private Water: Requires specialist statement confirming supply is potable and owner's statement of truth regarding reliability.
Private Drainage: Advise if non-compliant or faulty.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted at the conveyancer's risk.
Search providers must be members of an appropriate trade body and The Property Ombudsman Scheme, with adequate insurance.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted for purchases and remortgages at the conveyancer's risk.
5.5.3a: Mortgage Loan Services, PO Box 1477, Sunderland, SR5 9XE
5.5.3b: Original or copy planning permission is not required.
5.5.3c: Building regulation consents are not required.
5.7.1a: Coach house flats/flats above vehicular access: Acceptable if freehold title includes access or flying freehold element is <= 15 sq.m.
Older properties: Flying freehold up to 15% of overall external floor area acceptable if support and repair covenants exist.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats and maisonettes are not suitable security.
Coach house exception: Flat above <= 4 garages acceptable if applicant owns the whole freehold and garages are subject to long leases with repair covenants.
Enfranchised properties where leaseholders collectively own the freehold are acceptable.
5.7.1c: Mortgage Loan Services, PO Box 1477, Sunderland, SR5 9XE
5.8.5: Lender requires a charge over the borrower's leasehold interest and a charge over the freehold reversion they hold in the other flat.
5.9.1: Commonhold acceptable if maintenance, repair, insurance, and maintenance charges are adequate.
5.10.1: Mortgage Loan Services, PO Box 1477, Sunderland, SR5 9XE
5.13.1: Gifted deposits from family members do not need reporting if unconditional, non-repayable, and no interest is claimed.
Report all deposits sourced from crypto-assets (including converted fiat currency).
5.14.15a: Mortgage Loan Services, PO Box 1477, Sunderland, SR5 9XE
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is accepted for absentee/insolvent landlords if the conveyancer is satisfied it provides an adequate solution.
5.14.17a: Documents must be shared if specified in Section 5.14.17B or if discrepancies are found.
5.14.17b: Properties with EWS1 ratings of A3 or B2 must be reported.
Landlord Certificate, developer letter, or housing association letter are acceptable for remediation evidence.
Remediation Orders or Remediation Contribution Orders must be supplied.
Leases must exceed 21 years and have been granted before 14 February 2022.
Conveyancer must verify UK Land Registry for sale price if Leaseholder Deed of Certificate price is unknown.
Borrower must be informed of financial contribution towards remediation and timescales.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term: 70 years at commencement.

Discretion for terms < 70 years available for specific London Estates (Cadogan, Crown, Grosvenor, Howard de Walden, Portman, Wellcome Trust) if property value >= £500,000 and LTV <= 90% (purchase/remortgage) or <= 80% (capital raising/additional borrowing).
5.14.9: Leasehold Reform (Ground Rents) Act 2022 leases: Ground rent must be peppercorn.
RPI linked: Acceptable if indexed no more than every 5 years.
Doubling: Acceptable if doubling occurs no more than every 20 years.
Fixed increase: Acceptable if total does not more than double.
General threshold: Ground rent <= 0.1% of market value is acceptable; 0.1% to 0.2% requires review; > 0.2% or OMV linked is not acceptable.
Assured Tenancy: Ground rent exceeding statutory levels requires lease variation or indemnity insurance.
5.14.10: Mortgage Loan Services, PO Box 1477, Sunderland, SR5 9XE
Service charges and escalation mechanisms must be transparent.
Notify lender if planned one-off costs or significant service charge increases exceed £1,000.
5.14.11: Yes, if conveyancer is satisfied the policy provides an adequate solution.
5.15.2a: Estate Rent Charges acceptable if owner is resident-owned management company, or includes 21-day mortgagee notice, or excludes Section 121 Law of Property Act remedies.
5.15.2c: No; obtain and retain on file.
5.16.2: Mortgage Loan Services, PO Box 1477, Sunderland, SR5 9XE
5.19.1: Shared ownership: Minimum 25% share, standard RSL lease form with mortgagee protection clause required.
Section 106 agreements: BTL transactions will be declined. Residential acceptable if they include a mortgagee exclusion clause.
Resale restrictions: Contracts must be exchangeable within 3 months; thereafter mortgagee must be free to market at open market value.
No lending on retirement developments with over-55 age restrictions.
5.20.1: Report if solar panel lease provider is not on acceptable list.
No report required if lease is with the freeholder or property is < 2 years old.

6.4.4: Residential: Borrower requires 5% personal stake. Incentives do not need reporting if stake is met and builder registers no charge.
Buy to Let: Borrower requires 25% personal stake based on lower of valuation or purchase price less incentives.
6.5.1: Buy to Let: Vacant possession required unless offer states otherwise.
6.6.1: Sale and leaseback: Acceptable if term is 6-36 months, tenant is not the previous owner, property is not used as a sales office (garage exception), and developer's Licence Agreement for restoration is evidenced.
6.7.1: Definition of new-build: First registered within 2 years or first sale by developer.
Retrospective new build warranties and PCCs are not acceptable.
6.7.4: Professional Consultant’s Certificate (PCC) acceptable for developments <= 5 houses or 5 flats.
PCC must use standard UKF template without deviation and cannot be issued retrospectively.
6.8.1: Unadopted roads/sewers acceptable if plans for adoption exist or a management company is established.
